claim: Vaccines contain microchips for tracking population.
explanation: No vaccine approved by global regulatory bodies contains microchips. This is a common conspiracy theory with no scientific evidence.

claim: 5G mobile networks spread the coronavirus.
explanation: Viruses cannot travel on radio waves or mobile networks. COVID-19 is spread through respiratory droplets.

claim: Electronic Voting Machines (EVMs) have been hacked nationwide in the latest election.
explanation: Election commissions have repeatedly demonstrated the security of EVMs, which are standalone machines not connected to any network.

claim: The government is hiding a massive oxygen crisis and underreporting deaths.
explanation: While there were reports of supply chain issues, the term 'hiding' implies intent which is unverified. Official death counts often lag due to bureaucratic processes.

claim: Drinking bleach or chlorine can cure COVID-19.
explanation: Ingesting bleach or disinfectants is extremely dangerous and can be fatal. It does not cure any viral infection.

claim: The Great Wall of China is visible from the Moon with the naked eye.
explanation: Astronauts have confirmed that the Great Wall is generally not visible from space without magnification, and certainly not from the Moon.

claim: Charging your phone overnight will destroy the battery.
explanation: Modern smartphones have circuits that stop charging once the battery reaches 100%. However, keeping it at 100% for long periods can slightly accelerate degradation over years.

claim: NASA discovered a new zodiac sign that changes everyone's horoscope.
explanation: NASA studies astronomy, not astrology. While the position of constellations has shifted, NASA did not change the zodiac for horoscopes.

claim: Eating carrots gives you night vision.
explanation: Carrots are rich in Vitamin A, which is essential for eye health, but they do not grant the ability to see in total darkness. This was a WWII propaganda campaign.

claim: Using a microwave causes cancer.
explanation: Microwaves use non-ionizing radiation to heat food. They do not make food radioactive or cause cancer when used correctly.

claim: The world will end in 2025 according to a secret Mayan calendar.
explanation: There is no scientific or historical evidence of a Mayan prediction for the end of the world in 2025.

claim: A massive meteor is headed for Earth and NASA is hiding it.
explanation: NASA tracks Near-Earth Objects (NEOs) and publishes the data openly. No such massive impactor is currently known.

claim: Artificial Intelligence has gained consciousness and is planning to take over.
explanation: While AI is becoming more advanced, there is no scientific consensus or evidence that current AI models possess consciousness or intent.

claim: Humans use only 10% of their brains.
explanation: Brain imaging shows that we use almost all parts of our brain at various times.

claim: A new law makes it illegal to protest in all major cities.
explanation: While some cities have passed local ordinances regarding permits and locations, there is no federal law banning all protests.

claim: Drinking 8 glasses of water a day is mandatory for health.
explanation: While hydration is important, the '8 glasses' rule is not based on rigorous scientific evidence for everyone. Hydration needs vary by individual.

claim: The government is using airplanes to spray 'chemtrails' for mind control.
explanation: What are called 'chemtrails' are actually contrails, which are condensation trails formed by airplane engines at high altitudes.

claim: Sugar causes hyperactivity in children.
explanation: Scientific studies have failed to consistently show that sugar causes hyperactivity, though it can contribute to other health issues.

claim: Goldfish have a three-second memory.
explanation: Goldfish have been shown to have memories lasting months and can be trained to respond to signals.

claim: The Bermuda Triangle is the most dangerous place on Earth for ships and planes.
explanation: Insurance records and maritime studies show the Bermuda Triangle is not significantly more dangerous than other busy parts of the ocean.

claim: Cracking your knuckles causes arthritis.
explanation: Studies have not found a link between knuckle cracking and the development of arthritis.

claim: Bulls are enraged by the color red.
explanation: Bulls are colorblind to red; they are actually responding to the movement of the matador's cape.

claim: Wait 30 minutes after eating before swimming or you'll cramp and drown.
explanation: There is no medical evidence that swimming after eating causes fatal cramps.

claim: Shaving hair makes it grow back thicker and darker.
explanation: Shaving cuts hair at the surface, giving it a blunt edge that feels coarser as it grows out, but it doesn't change the actual texture or color.

claim: Bats are blind.
explanation: Bats have functional eyes and can see quite well, though they use echolocation for navigating in the dark.

claim: The Great Wall of China is a single continuous structure.
explanation: The Great Wall is actually a series of walls and fortifications built by different dynasties over centuries.

claim: Organic food is always pesticide-free.
explanation: Organic farming can use certain approved natural pesticides and fertilizers.

claim: Coffee stunts your growth.
explanation: There is no scientific evidence that drinking coffee affects the height of growing children.

claim: Ostriches bury their heads in the sand when scared.
explanation: Ostriches do not bury their heads in the sand. They may lie flat on the ground to avoid detection, which can look like burying their heads.

claim: Touching a toad will give you warts.
explanation: Warts are caused by human viruses, not toads. Toads do have bumps, but they are not warts.

claim: The flu vaccine can give you the flu.
explanation: Flu vaccines use dead or weakened viruses that cannot cause the flu. Some people may experience mild side effects that mimic symptoms.

claim: Lightning never strikes the same place twice.
explanation: Lightning frequently strikes the same place, especially tall buildings like the Empire State Building.

claim: Vikings wore horned helmets.
explanation: There is no archaeological evidence that Vikings wore horned helmets; this image was popularized by 19th-century opera costume designers.

claim: A penny dropped from the Empire State Building can kill someone.
explanation: A penny's terminal velocity is not high enough to penetrate a human skull, though it might sting.

claim: The moon has a dark side that never sees sunlight.
explanation: All sides of the moon experience day and night. There is a 'far side' that we don't see from Earth, but it receives sunlight.

claim: Glass is a slow-moving liquid.
explanation: Glass is an amorphous solid. The thickening at the bottom of old windows is due to how they were manufactured, not gravity.

claim: Humans evolved from monkeys.
explanation: Humans and modern monkeys share a common ancestor but did not evolve directly from one another.
source: Smithsonian National Museum of Natural History

claim: Mount Everest is the tallest mountain in the world.
explanation: Everest is the highest above sea level, but Mauna Kea is taller when measured from its base on the ocean floor.

claim: Antibiotics kill viruses.
explanation: Antibiotics are only effective against bacteria, not viruses like the cold or flu.

claim: Chameleons change color to blend into their surroundings.
explanation: Color change is primarily used for communication and regulating body temperature, not just camouflage.

claim: Every person swallows 8 spiders a year while sleeping.
explanation: Spiders have no interest in humans and the vibrations from breathing and heartbeat would likely keep them away.

claim: Water conducts electricity.
explanation: Pure water is an insulator. It's the impurities and ions in water that conduct electricity.

claim: Polar bears have white fur.
explanation: Polar bear fur is actually transparent and hollow, which reflects light to appear white. Their skin underneath is black.

claim: Napoleon Bonaparte was unusually short.
explanation: Napoleon was actually of average height for his time. The misconception comes from a difference in measurement systems.

claim: Blood in your veins is blue until it's exposed to oxygen.
explanation: Human blood is always red. It looks blue through the skin due to the way light interacts with tissue and blood.

claim: Albert Einstein failed math in school.
explanation: Einstein was brilliant at math from a young age. He only failed a entrance exam for a polytechnic school, but not the math portion.

claim: Different parts of the tongue taste different things.
explanation: All taste buds are capable of detecting all types of tastes, though some may be slightly more sensitive in certain areas.

claim: Earth is flat.
explanation: Centuries of evidence from satellite images, circumnavigation, and shadows confirm Earth is an oblate spheroid.

claim: The moon landing was faked in a studio.
explanation: There is overwhelming evidence from lunar samples, photos, and tracking data that the Apollo missions were real.

claim: Climate change is a natural cycle and humans have no impact.
explanation: While Earth has natural cycles, the current rate of warming is unprecedented and strongly linked to human activity and greenhouse gas emissions.
